name: django-revproxy

metadata.name: django-revproxy
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Mozilla Public License 2.0 (MPL 2.0)
metadata.summary: Yet another Django reverse proxy application

documentation:
# Welcome to django-revproxy

```{image} https://jazzband.co/static/img/badge.svg
:alt: Jazzband
:target: https://jazzband.co/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/django-revproxy.svg
:alt: PyPI version
:target: https://pypi.org/project/django-revproxy/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/django-revproxy.svg
:alt: Supported Python versions
:target: https://pypi.org/project/django-revproxy/
```

```{image} https://github.com/jazzband/django-revproxy/workflows/Test/badge.svg
:alt: GitHub Actions
:target: https://github.com/jazzband/django-revproxy/actions
```

```{image} https://codecov.io/gh/jazzband/django-revproxy/branch/master/graph/badge.svg
:alt: Test Coverage
:target: https://codecov.io/gh/jazzband/django-revproxy
```

A simple reverse proxy using Django. It allows to use Django as a
reverse Proxy to HTTP requests. It also allows to use Django as an
authentication Proxy.

Documentation available at <http://django-revproxy.readthedocs.org/>

## Features

- Proxies all HTTP methods: HEAD, GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS, TRACE, CONNECT and PATCH
- Copy all http headers sent from the client to the proxied server
- Copy all http headers sent from the proxied server to the client (except [hop-by-hop](http://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616-sec13.html#sec13.5.1))
- Basic URL rewrite
- Sets the http header REMOTE_USER if the user is logged in Django
- Sets the http headers X-Forwarded-For and X-Forwarded-Proto
- Handles redirects
- Few external dependencies
- Apply XSLT transformation in the response (requires Diazo)

## Dependencies

- django >= 3.0
- urllib3 >= 1.12
- diazo >= 1.0.5 (optional)
- lxml >= 3.4, < 3.5 (optional, but diazo dependency)

## Install

`pip install django-revproxy`

functions.name: revproxy.transformer.asbool
documentation:
Function used to convert certain string values into an appropriated
boolean value.If value is not a string the built-in python
bool function will be used to convert the passed parameter

:param      value: an object to be converted to a boolean value
:returns:   A boolean value

classes.name: revproxy.transformer.DiazoTransformer

methods.name: revproxy.transformer.DiazoTransformer.is_ajax
documentation:
request.is_ajax() is marked as deprecated in django 3.1. removed in 4.0
See: https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/3.1/releases/3.1/#id2

methods.name: revproxy.transformer.DiazoTransformer.should_transform
documentation:
Determine if we should transform the response

:returns:  A boolean value

methods.name: revproxy.transformer.DiazoTransformer.transform
documentation:
Method used to make a transformation on the content of
the http response based on the rules and theme_templates
passed as paremters

:param  rules: A file with a set of diazo rules to make a
               transformation over the original response content
:param  theme_template: A file containing the template used to format
                        the the original response content
:param    is_html5: A boolean parameter to identify a html5 doctype
:returns: A response with a content transformed based on the rules and
          theme_template

methods.name: revproxy.transformer.DiazoTransformer.reset_headers
documentation:
This method remove the header Content-Length entry
from the response

methods.name: revproxy.transformer.DiazoTransformer.set_html5_doctype
documentation: Method used to transform a doctype in to a properly html5 doctype
Class used to make a diazo transformation on a request or a response

functions.name: revproxy.utils.is_html_content_type
documentation:
Function used to verify if the parameter is a proper html content type

```
param content_type

String variable that represent a content-type

returns

A boolean value stating if the content_type is a valid html
content type
```

functions.name: revproxy.utils.should_stream
documentation:
Function to verify if the proxy_response must be converted into
a stream.This will be done by checking the proxy_response content-length
and verify if its length is bigger than one stipulated
by MIN_STREAMING_LENGTH.

```
param proxy_response

An Instance of urllib3.response.HTTPResponse

returns

A boolean stating if the proxy_response should
be treated as a stream
```

functions.name: revproxy.utils.get_charset
documentation:
Function used to retrieve the charset from a content-type.If there is no
charset in the content type then the charset defined on DEFAULT_CHARSET
will be returned

:param  content_type:   A string containing a Content-Type header
:returns:               A string containing the charset

functions.name: revproxy.utils.required_header
documentation:
Function that verify if the header parameter is an essential header

```
param header

A string represented a header

returns

A boolean value that represent if the header is required
```

functions.name: revproxy.utils.normalize_request_headers
documentation:
Function used to transform header, replacing 'HTTP\_' to ''
and replace '\_' to '-'

```
param request

A HttpRequest that will be transformed

returns

A dictionary with the normalized headers
```

functions.name: revproxy.utils.encode_items
documentation:
Function that encode all elements in the list of items passed as
a parameter

```
param items

A list of tuple

returns

A list of tuple with all items encoded in 'utf-8'
```

functions.name: revproxy.utils.cookie_from_string
documentation:
Parser for HTTP header set-cookie
The return from this function will be used as parameters for
django's response.set_cookie method. Because set_cookie doesn't
have parameter comment, this cookie attribute will be ignored.

:param  cookie_string: A string representing a valid cookie
:param  strict_cookies: Whether to only accept RFC-compliant cookies
:returns: A dictionary containing the cookie_string attributes

functions.name: revproxy.utils.unquote
documentation:
Remove wrapping quotes from a string.

```
param value

A string that might be wrapped in double quotes, such
as a HTTP cookie value.

returns

Beginning and ending quotes removed and escaped quotes (")
unescaped
```

variables.name: revproxy.utils.MIN_STREAMING_LENGTH
documentation: 4KB

classes.name: revproxy.exceptions.InvalidUpstream

modules.name: revproxy.views
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: revproxy.views.ProxyView

methods.name: revproxy.views.ProxyView.get_proxy_request_headers
documentation:
Get normalized headers for the upstream

Gets all headers from the original request and normalizes them.
Normalization occurs by removing the prefix `HTTP_` and
replacing and `_` by `-`. Example: `HTTP_ACCEPT_ENCODING`
becames `Accept-Encoding`.

:::{versionadded} 0.9.1
:::

```
param request

The original HTTPRequest instance

returns

Normalized headers for the upstream
```

methods.name: revproxy.views.ProxyView.get_request_headers
documentation:
Return request headers that will be sent to upstream.

The header REMOTE_USER is set to the current user
if AuthenticationMiddleware is enabled and
the view's add_remote_user property is True.

:::{versionadded} 0.9.8
:::

If the view's add_x_forwarded property is True, the
headers X-Forwarded-For and X-Forwarded-Proto are set to the
IP address of the requestor and the request's protocol (http or https),
respectively.

:::{versionadded} TODO
:::

methods.name: revproxy.views.ProxyView.get_quoted_path
documentation: Return quoted path to be used in proxied request

methods.name: revproxy.views.ProxyView.get_encoded_query_params
documentation: Return encoded query params to be used in proxied request

class_variables.name: revproxy.views.ProxyView.rewrite
documentation: It will be overrided by a tuple inside tuple.

View responsable by excute proxy requests, process and return
their responses.

functions.name: revproxy.connection._output
documentation: Host header should always be first

functions.name: revproxy.response.get_django_response
documentation:
This method is used to create an appropriate response based on the
Content-Length of the proxy_response. If the content is bigger than
MIN_STREAMING_LENGTH, which is found on utils.py,
than django.http.StreamingHttpResponse will be created,
else a django.http.HTTPResponse will be created instead

```
param proxy_response

An Instance of urllib3.response.HTTPResponse that
will create an appropriate response

param strict_cookies

Whether to only accept RFC-compliant cookies

param streaming_amount

The amount for streaming HTTP response, if not
given, use a dynamic value -- 1("no-buffering")
for "text/event-stream" content type, 65535 for
other types.

returns

Returns an appropriate response based on the proxy_response
content-length
```

functions.name: revproxy.response.get_streaming_amt
documentation:
Get the value of streaming amount(in bytes) when streaming response

```
param proxy_response

urllib3.response.HTTPResponse object
```
